title: SmartDesk AI
shortDescription: AI-powered ITSM platform that resolves tier-1 tickets autonomously.
fullDescription:
SmartDesk AI is an enterprise-grade IT service management platform that uses LLMs and retrieval-augmented generation to resolve tier-1 tickets without human intervention. Built for a banking environment where speed and auditability both matter.

The system ingests historical ticket data, runbooks, and knowledge base articles into a vector store. When a new ticket arrives, an agent classifies it, retrieves the most relevant runbook, drafts a response, and either auto-resolves or escalates to a human with full context. Every action is logged for audit.

I designed the architecture end-to-end: the Next.js operator console, the FastAPI orchestration layer, the Postgres + pgvector store, and the CI/CD pipeline that ships models behind feature flags.
architecture: Next.js 15 frontend on Vercel → FastAPI orchestration on ECS Fargate → Postgres + pgvector on RDS → S3 for document storage. Async event bus via SQS for ticket ingestion.
lessonsLearned: The biggest lesson was treating the LLM as an unreliable component. Every prompt is wrapped in retry logic with structured outputs, and the system degrades gracefully to human-only when the model fails or returns low-confidence answers.
challenges: Hallucination on edge-case tickets was the hard problem. Solved it with a combination of constrained decoding, RAG over runbooks, and a confidence threshold that forces human review below ~0.7.
businessImpact: Resolved 42% of tier-1 tickets fully autonomously in the first month of pilot. Reduced average resolution time from 4.2 hours to 38 minutes for handled tickets.

media.caption: Operator console with real-time ticket queue

title: Banking Mobile App Redesign
shortDescription: Mobile-first banking experience with biometric auth and offline mode.
fullDescription:
Redesigned and rebuilt the customer-facing mobile banking app from scratch. Native-quality UX with biometric login, offline transaction queuing, and a design system that ships features in days, not weeks.

The architecture uses React Native with a thin native layer for biometric flows and secure storage. Offline transactions are queued in SQLite and synced via a conflict-resolution protocol when connectivity returns. The design system is a single source of truth used across web and mobile.
architecture: React Native app → REST/GraphQL gateway → Node.js services → Postgres primary with read replicas. Biometric auth via native bridge. Secure storage using Keychain (iOS) and EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android).
lessonsLearned: Building a real offline-first app is mostly about designing the sync model correctly upfront. We spent more time on conflict resolution than on the UI — and that was the right call.
challenges: Compliance: every transaction needs to be auditable end-to-end, and offline mode makes that non-trivial. Solved with a signed transaction queue that is replayed in order when online.
businessImpact: Increased mobile DAU by 64% in the first quarter after launch. App store rating went from 2.8 to 4.6.

title: Change Risk AI
shortDescription: ML model that scores change requests by risk before they hit production.
fullDescription:
Change Risk AI is a machine learning service that scores every change request submitted to the CAB on a 0–100 risk scale, with explanations. Trained on five years of historical change records, incidents, and post-mortems.

The model is a gradient-boosted tree ensemble wrapped in a FastAPI service, with a SHAP-based explanation layer so change managers can see *why* a change is flagged. A monitoring dashboard tracks calibration drift and retrains automatically when accuracy drops below threshold.
architecture: FastAPI inference service on ECS → feature store in Postgres → training pipeline on SageMaker. SHAP explanations computed at inference time and surfaced in the Next.js CAB console.
lessonsLearned: Explainability mattered more than raw accuracy. Change managers wouldn't act on the score unless they understood the reason. SHAP was non-negotiable.
challenges: Class imbalance (most changes are low-risk). Solved with SMOTE + cost-sensitive learning and a calibration layer that maps raw scores to a 0–100 scale.
businessImpact: Reduced change failure rate from 12% to 5% within six months. CAB meetings cut from 90 to 45 minutes.

media.caption: Risk scoring dashboard with SHAP explanations

title: OpsBoard
shortDescription: Executive SLA dashboard for IT operations — built to be used, not just admired.
fullDescription:
OpsBoard is the executive-facing SLA dashboard for the IT org. Pulls data from ServiceNow, Grafana, and incident logs, and surfaces the metrics leadership actually cares about: availability, MTTR, change failure rate, top recurring incidents.

Designed to be the first tab opened in the morning — fast, dense, and honest. Built with Next.js and a streaming data layer so charts update in real time during incidents.
architecture: Next.js dashboard → BFF aggregating ServiceNow + Grafana APIs → Postgres cache → WebSocket stream for live incident updates.
lessonsLearned: An executive dashboard only works if it shows *one* thing per chart. Resist the urge to cram.
challenges: Different data sources had different time zones and update cadences. Built a normalization layer that anchors everything to UTC and reconciles updates with a watermark.
businessImpact: Adopted across the IT org. Cited as the single most useful internal tool in the next quarterly survey.

title: KB Semantic Search
shortDescription: Semantic search over the IT knowledge base — find the runbook in seconds.
fullDescription:
Semantic search over an internal IT knowledge base of ~5,000 articles. Replaced keyword search with a sentence-transformer-based dense retrieval system. Users describe the problem in natural language and get the most relevant runbook, not just the one with the right keywords.

Built with sentence-transformers, FAISS, and a Next.js search UI that surfaces results with the matching section highlighted.
architecture: sentence-transformers indexer runs nightly → FAISS index stored in S3 → FastAPI retrieval service → Next.js UI.
lessonsLearned: Hybrid search (BM25 + dense) beat pure dense retrieval by a wide margin. Keyword signals still matter, especially for error codes and product names.
challenges: Keeping the index fresh without rebuilding from scratch nightly. Used a delta-update pipeline that ingests only changed articles.
businessImpact: Average time to find a runbook dropped from 6 minutes to 28 seconds. Self-service rate on tier-1 issues increased by 31%.

title: DevTooling CLI
shortDescription: Open-source CLI that automates the boring parts of shipping software.
fullDescription:
An open-source command-line tool that automates the boring parts of shipping software: scaffolding, environment checks, deployment previews, and post-deploy verification. Written in Go for fast startup, distributed as a single static binary.

Used by ~2,000 developers across multiple teams. Featured on the GitHub trending list for a week.
architecture: Single Go binary → pluggable subcommands → GitHub Action for CI integration.
lessonsLearned: Documentation matters more than features. Adding clear examples tripled weekly active users.
challenges: Cross-platform packaging without CI headaches. Settled on GoReleaser after two false starts.
